text: The laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass procedure is set up with six ports visible on the patient's abdomen, facilitating the use of two hands by both the surgeon and the first assistant during dissection.
The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to divide the greater omentum, reducing tension on the Roux limb during the anticholic-antigastric approach of the Roux-en-Y gastric bypass procedure.
